resource.name: sessions/8939442334574563036
resource.title: Detecting Internet Access in a Sandboxed Environment
resource.prompt: it's been a minute, but i want to see how much of this works. in a fully locked sand.. somehting locked env. not itnernet access. first tell me how to tell if anyhting is tring to access internet.

gitPatch.suggestedCommitMessage: Add test script and Dockerfile for isolated network testing

pullRequest.title: Add script to test sandbox network access
pullRequest.description:
Added a Node.js script `test_network.js` to test inbound and outbound network connectivity. This verifies whether the sandbox environment restricts internet access.

The script:
1.  Tests outbound connectivity by sending an HTTPS GET request to `https://google.com` and logging the result (success, error, or timeout).
2.  Tests inbound connectivity by starting a local HTTP server bound to `0.0.0.0` and sending a test request to itself.

---
*PR created automatically by Jules for task [8939442334574563036](https://jules.google.com/task/8939442334574563036) started by @A517-AB*
